skill.name: email-and-password-best-practices
skill.description: Configure email verification, implement password reset flows, set password policies, and customise hashing algorithms for Better Auth email/password authentication. Use when users need to set up login, sign-in, sign-up, credential authentication, or password security with Better Auth.
skill.summary: Configure Better Auth email/password authentication with verification, password reset flows, and custom password hashing.
skill.author: better-auth

security_audit.summary: All 52 static detections are false positives. The 'external_commands' flags are markdown inline code formatting (backticks). 'Network' flags are example URLs in documentation. 'Weak crypto' and 'system reconnaissance' flags misidentified security library references. The skill is legitimate Better Auth documentation recommending SCRYPT (default) and Argon2id hashing. No malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Configure Better Auth Email and Password Authentication
content.value_statement: Setting up secure email/password authentication requires careful configuration of verification flows, password reset mechanisms, and hashing algorithms. This skill provides configuration examples and security best practices for Better Auth.
actual_capabilities: Enable and configure email/password authentication in Better Auth
Set up email verification with custom email templates
Implement password reset flows with secure token handling
Switch between SCRYPT and Argon2id password hashing algorithms
Enable session revocation on password reset
limitations: Requires existing Better Auth setup - cannot initialize a new project
Email sending requires your own email service integration
Password hash migration requires user re-authentication
Requires server-side implementation for client-side examples

use_cases.title: Add email verification to user registration
use_cases.description: Configure Better Auth to send verification emails when users sign up, requiring email confirmation before account activation.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers setting up new user registration

use_cases.title: Implement password reset functionality
use_cases.description: Add secure password reset flows with time-limited tokens, background email sending, and session revocation options.

use_cases.title: Configure secure password hashing
use_cases.description: Switch from default SCRYPT to Argon2id for enhanced password security with configurable memory and iteration parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Enable email/password authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Enable email and password authentication in Better Auth with email verification. Show me the configuration with sendVerificationEmail callback.

prompt_templates.title: Add password reset flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a password reset flow to our Better Auth setup. Include sendResetPassword callback, token expiration config, and session revocation option.

prompt_templates.title: Configure password requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure password requirements in Better Auth - minimum 12 characters, maximum 256 characters. Also add requireEmailVerification to block unverified users.

prompt_templates.title: Switch to Argon2id hashing
prompt_templates.prompt: Switch our Better Auth password hashing from SCRYPT to Argon2id using @node-rs/argon2. Show the configuration with recommended memory cost, time cost, and parallelism settings.

output_examples.output: Configuration object with emailAndPassword.enabled, emailVerification.sendVerificationEmail callback that receives user, url, and token parameters
Configure sendResetPassword callback, set resetPasswordTokenExpiresIn to 1800 seconds, enable revokeSessionsOnPasswordReset

output_examples.input: Set up Argon2id with secure defaults
output_examples.output: Hash function configuration with memoryCost: 65536, timeCost: 3, parallelism: 4, and algorithm: 2 for Argon2id variant

best_practices: Always use absolute URLs (including origin) for callback URLs to prevent domain inference issues
Configure background task handlers on serverless platforms to ensure email delivery
Enable revokeSessionsOnPasswordReset to invalidate all sessions after password change
anti_patterns: Do not use relative URLs for callbacks - this causes issues when backend and frontend are on different domains
Do not skip email verification requirement for high-security applications
Do not switch password hashing algorithms without a migration strategy

faq.question: What is the default password hashing algorithm in Better Auth?
faq.answer: Better Auth uses SCRYPT by default, which is a memory-hard algorithm included in Node.js without external dependencies.

faq.question: How do I switch from SCRYPT to Argon2id?
faq.answer: Provide custom hash and verify functions using @node-rs/argon2 in your emailAndPassword configuration with the password object.

faq.question: What happens to existing users if I switch hashing algorithms?
faq.answer: Users with passwords hashed under the old algorithm cannot sign in. You must implement a migration strategy or force password reset.

faq.question: How long are password reset tokens valid?
faq.answer: Tokens expire after 1 hour by default. Configure resetPasswordTokenExpiresIn in seconds to change this duration.

faq.question: Do password reset tokens get invalidated after use?
faq.answer: Yes, tokens are single-use. They are deleted immediately after a successful password reset.

faq.question: Why should I configure a background task handler for serverless?
faq.answer: Serverless functions may terminate before email sending completes. Background handlers like waitUntil ensure emails are sent reliably.
